purpose: Assesses wage-price spiral risk by requiring all necessary institutional conditions to be met simultaneously before declaring spiral risk elevated
components: Condition C: Firm pricing power sufficient for full cost pass-through
Condition D: Expectations de-anchoring above Coibion-Gorodnichenko threshold
Institutional prerequisites: union density >25%, COLA coverage >60%
Historical validation: true spirals only in 1966-69 and 1973-75 when all conditions met
current_assessment: Currently 0/3-4 conditions met. Union density ~10%, COLA coverage <10%. The 2021-23 natural experiment (2/3 conditions temporarily met) confirmed the conjunctive requirement. Spiral probability <10-15%. However, inflation can persist through non-spiral channels.
limitations: Does not address non-spiral persistence channels (fiscal, commodity, shelter, expectations drift)
Absence of spiral risk does not imply inflation returns to target
Institutional prerequisites may evolve over long horizons
